codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 6 required functions plus a bonus buildFollowUpPrompt helper. The code follows existing project conventions (Supabase client injection, ESM imports, error throwing pattern, TypeScript strict mode). Types are properly defined in types.ts (ConversationSession, SessionState). Tests are comprehensive with 31 cases covering happy paths, error propagation, edge cases, and lifecycle integration. The mock pattern is consistent with the project's existing helpers.ts approach. All validation contracts (VAL-SESSION-001 through VAL-SESSION-004 and VAL-EDGE-003) are addressed.

issues.description: expireOldSessions docstring says 'last_inbound_at (or updated_at as fallback)' but the implementation exclusively uses `updated_at` in the `.lt('updated_at', cutoff)` query. The DB migration (003_conversation_sessions.sql) creates an index `idx_conversation_sessions_last_inbound` on `last_inbound_at` that will never be used by this function. Either the docstring should be corrected to say 'updated_at', or the implementation should use `last_inbound_at` (which would match the index). Using `updated_at` is arguably better since the DB trigger auto-updates it on any change, but the doc/code/index mismatch is confusing.

Test 'should not affect one session when resolving another' only verifies that resolveSession was called with session-1's ID. It does not actually verify that session-2 remains unresolved/accessible — it just checks the update call arguments. The test title overpromises what is actually being asserted. A stronger test would mock a subsequent getActiveSessions call and verify session-2 is still returned.

findSessionForReply signature omits userId compared to the feature spec which says 'findSessionForReply(userId, replyText, activeSessions)'. The omission is justified since the function is pure (operates on pre-fetched sessions already filtered by user), but callers must ensure they pass user-filtered sessions. This is a reasonable design simplification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker tried to invoke 'backend-worker' skill which doesn't exist (no .factory/skills directory). Worker fell back to 'mission-worker-base' which was sufficient. The feature's skillName field is set to 'backend-worker' but this skill was never created.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Error: Skill "backend-worker" not found'. skillFeedback.suggestedChanges confirms: 'The backend-worker skill was not found; the mission-worker-base skill was sufficient for this feature.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The expireOldSessions function uses `updated_at` for staleness detection, but the DB migration creates an index on `last_inbound_at` for cleanup queries (idx_conversation_sessions_last_inbound). There's no documented convention about which timestamp column to use for session staleness. This could cause confusion for future features that interact with session expiry.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: supabase/migrations/003_conversation_sessions.sql line 38: 'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversation_sessions_last_inbound ON conversation_sessions(last_inbound_at)' vs agent/src/sessions.ts line 153: '.lt("updated_at", cutoff)'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Supabase mock pattern (chainable query builder with thennable resolution) is a non-trivial codebase-specific pattern that new workers need to understand. It's implemented in helpers.ts but not documented anywhere. Workers need to know to use `mockReturnValue({ data, error, then: ... })` for terminal methods.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: agent/src/__tests__/helpers.ts createMockSupabaseClient() — used consistently across all test files but not documented in AGENTS.md or library/

summary: The conversation-sessions feature is well-implemented with clean code that follows project conventions. All 6 required functions (createSession, getActiveSessions, resolveSession, updateSession, findSessionForReply, expireOldSessions) plus a buildFollowUpPrompt helper are correctly implemented. The 31 tests comprehensively cover all validation contracts (VAL-SESSION-001 through 004, VAL-EDGE-003). Three non-blocking issues found: (1) docstring/index mismatch in expireOldSessions regarding which timestamp column is used for staleness, (2) one test title overpromises its assertions, (3) findSessionForReply signature differs from spec (justified simplification). No blocking issues — feature passes review.
